errors.note: Cloudflare 524 — vendor backend exceeds 100s edge timeout. Confirmed both flows (per-row inline-blob with isResTable:true, AND bulk async-via-email) hit the same wall. Bulk-async target email on this demo tenant is the placeholder 'Demoville1@1.com', so async delivery is unrecoverable. PDF/EXCEL/KML for the dashboard data are client-side builds in the SPA (functions IUe/jUe/hZe) and can be regenerated offline from the harvester's JSON dumps without any server call — only PHOTOS and LEAK REPORT actually need the vendor backend.
Cloudflare 524 — same root cause. The demo dataset has no leak photos uploaded so the zip would have been empty regardless (per-leak /api/utilis-finding-photos returned null for all 2 inspections).

wms.note: Hosted on the separately-branded satellite-provider Map Viewer GIS Cloud tenant at utilis.giscloud.com. Auth is form login on giscloud.com; the same demo creds happen to work but that's coincidence (no SSO from EO-Discover). The SPA's WMS button only copies the URL into a popup — no credential handoff. GetCapabilities returns a single composite Layer 'Demoville A' (no Name attribute, so per-layer GetMap by name is rejected with LayerNotDefined). Supported CRS: EPSG:4326/3857/900913. GetMap formats: image/png, image/png8, image/jpeg. Max 2048x2048. Internal composition (visible in giscloud's native layer tree, not WMS): Field Results (id 7691538: Leak/Suspected/Repaired Leak), POIs - DR2 (id 7691544: ERA/Overlapping/Active Segment/Investigated Segment), Pipes (id 7691554), and Mapbox basemaps. WMS = operator cartography for QGIS/ArcMap users, not a programmatic API.
